deprecations.replacement: before_filter/after_filter/around_filter were deprecated in Rails 4.0 and removed in Rails 5.1. Replace with before_action/after_action/around_action — same arguments and semantics.
update_attributes/update_attributes! were deprecated in Rails 6.1 and removed in Rails 7.0. Use .update / .update! — identical signature, bang variant raises on validation failure.
ActiveRecord::Relation#uniq was deprecated in Rails 5.0 and removed in Rails 5.1. Use .distinct on relations. (Array#uniq is unchanged.)
render text: was deprecated in Rails 4.1 and removed in Rails 5.1. Use render plain: for plain-text bodies (sets Content-Type: text/plain). Use render html: for trusted HTML strings.
render nothing: true was deprecated in Rails 5.1 and removed in Rails 5.2. Use head :ok (or head :no_content) for empty responses — same status, no body, correct Content-Length.

redirect_to :back was deprecated in Rails 5.0 and removed in Rails 5.1. Use redirect_back(fallback_location: root_path) — identical when Referer is present, falls back to the given location otherwise (avoids ActionController::RedirectBackError).

Rails.application.secrets was deprecated in Rails 7.2 (planned removal in 8.1). Use Rails.application.credentials.dig(:section, :key) — encrypted at rest via config/credentials.yml.enc + config/master.key (or RAILS_MASTER_KEY env var). Edit with bin/rails credentials:edit.

Sprockets was replaced by Propshaft as the default asset pipeline in Rails 7+. Propshaft skips compilation/preprocessing — JS/CSS bundling delegated to Importmap (default), jsbundling-rails, or cssbundling-rails. Migrate by removing sprockets-rails from Gemfile, deleting app/assets/config/manifest.js, and configuring config.assets.paths via Propshaft.

ActiveJob :async adapter is for development/test only — runs jobs in-process and loses them on restart. Rails 8 default for production is solid_queue (DB-backed, no Redis required). Set config.active_job.queue_adapter = :solid_queue in config/environments/production.rb.

MemoryStore is per-process and not shared across workers — unsafe for production. Rails 8 default is :solid_cache_store (DB-backed, replaces Memcached/Redis for most apps). For higher throughput swap to :mem_cache_store or :redis_cache_store.

ActionCable :async adapter is for development/test only. Rails 8 default for production is solid_cable (DB-polling, no Redis required). For >100 concurrent subscribers swap to redis. Configure in config/cable.yml.
TextHelper#reset_cycle (and #cycle) were deprecated in Rails 6.1 and removed in Rails 7.0. Track cycle state explicitly in the view (e.g. with index_with or each_with_index) instead.
